questionText: Is your organization able to break down your emissions data for any of the subsidiaries included in your CDP response?
questionKo: 귀사는 CDP 응답에 포함된 자회사의 배출량 데이터를 세분화할 수 있습니까?
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: Awareness of subsidiary-level emission figures enables a parent company to better target action to reduce emissions. The breakdown also provides investors and other data users with the opportunity of better understanding the emissions sources and therefore the risks and opportunities throughout the business.
baseOptions: Not relevant as we do not have any subsidiaries

guidance.text:
General 
The "consolidation approach" (reported in 6.1) identifies which entities are included within the boundary that you are using for your disclosure of climate-related data (i.e. financial control, operational control, equity share or other).
Select "Yes" if your responses to questions 7.6, 7.7 and 7.8 include emissions data from subsidiaries, based on the approach selected in 6.1, and you are able to provide a breakdown of these emissions by subsidiary. If you select "Yes" you will be asked to break down your Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by subsidiary in the subsequent question. 
Select "No" if your responses to questions 7.6, 7.7, and 7.8 include emissions data from subsidiaries, but you are not able to breakdown your emissions data by subsidiary. Note that if the subsidiaries fall within the boundary determined by your consolidation approach and you are not including emissions data from the subsidiaries in your responses to 7.6, 7.7 and 7.8, you should disclose the subsidiaries as exclusions in 7.4.1. 
Select "Not relevant as we do not have subsidiaries" if there are no subsidiaries within the boundary determined by your consolidation approach.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 해당 문항에서 CDP가 요청하는 작성 내용을 설명합니다. 응답방법, 열 구조, 드롭다운 선택지를 기준으로 보고연도와 조직 범위에 맞는 정보를 입력하십시오.

guidance.text: Companies are transparent about their emissions inventories, including their subsidiaries.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항 응답에 필요한 추가 작성 기준을 설명합니다. 응답방법과 선택지를 함께 확인하여 작성하십시오.

guidance.text:
Explanation of terms
Subsidiary: a company owned or controlled by a parent company or holding company.
Consolidation approach: the identification of entities (companies, businesses, other groups, etc.) relevant to the environmental impact of the responding organization. The GHG Protocol states that two distinct approaches may be used to consolidate GHG emissions; the equity share and the control approaches. Control can be defined in either financial (financial control) or operational (operational control) terms.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항에서 사용하는 주요 용어의 의미를 설명합니다. 용어 해석은 CDP 2026 원문과 국문 양식의 용어 사용을 우선 적용하십시오.

sectorTagsKo: 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트,, &, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: 이 문항은 '귀사는 CDP 응답에 포함된 자회사의 배출량 데이터를 세분화할 수 있습니까? `'에 대한 배경 설명입니다. 응답자는 보고연도 기준으로 조직의 환경 관련 현황, 리스크, 기회, 성과 또는 관리체계를 문항의 응답 구조에 맞춰 설명해야 합니다.
